D5 Design L5 Evaluate Reference

OKLCH Harmony Wheel

spin a base hue and drop a harmony polygon onto the perceptual wheel — complementary, triadic, analogous, and more

Classic colour harmonies are simple polygons inscribed in a hue wheel. Built on OKLCH rather than HSL, equal hue steps stay perceptually even, so a triad really reads as three equal-tension colours instead of a lopsided set.

Set the base hue, shared lightness, and chroma, then choose a scheme. The polygon marks the member hues on the wheel and the swatch row is your palette. Out-of-gamut members are struck through.
